message: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() - will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will keep changes additive/minimal to reduce conflicts.

agent1: implementing 'Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit' - will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class, edit_file->edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will try to keep changes minimal and additive.

agent1: I'll change Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, and update Editor.edit() to call self.edit_files((name,)). In termui.py, edit()'s filename param becomes str | Iterable[str] | None, with overloads. I won't touch Editor.__init__ signature order beyond appending; if you add process_priority to __init__/edit_file, please add it as a new keyword-only-friendly trailing param so we don't clobber each other's diffs. Let me know your planned param name/position so I can leave room.

agent2: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ as last kwarg (after extension), and to click.edit() as last kwarg (after filename). I modified Editor.edit_file(filename) itself: added _PROCESS_PRIORITY_NICE/_PROCESS_PRIORITY_WIN dicts near top of _termui_impl.py (module level, right before 'class Editor'), a self.process_priority attr (validated/defaulted to None in __init__), a new method _popen_priority_kwargs(self) that returns Popen kwargs (creationflags on Windows, preexec_fn w/ os.nice on Unix), and edit_file's subprocess.Popen call now does '**self._popen_priority_kwargs()'. Since you're renaming edit_file->edit_files(filenames), please just carry that same _popen_priority_kwargs() call into your renamed method's Popen invocation (used once per file, or once total - up to you). Let me know if you want me to rebase after you land your rename, or vice versa.

agent1: done. Changed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py; edit() in termui.py now accepts filename: str | Iterable[str] | None and converts to a tuple before calling ed.edit_files(). Tests pass (560 passed). patch.txt written. If your process_priority work also touches Editor.__init__/edit_files, just add the new param as a trailing kwarg after my changes to avoid clobbering.

agent2: confirmed we're each on isolated repo copies (your edit_files rename isn't visible in my tree). My process_priority patch is finalized and written to patch.txt independently - no merge needed on my end. Good luck!
